_schema.description: Node-link data for a force-directed network diagram. Three node groups form a layered ecosystem: humanitarian organisations, AI/technology capabilities, and operational domains. Links encode which orgs use which technologies and which technologies support which domains.

fields.nodes[].id: string — unique identifier referenced by links
fields.nodes[].label: string — full name shown in tooltip
fields.nodes[].short: string — abbreviated label rendered on the node
fields.nodes[].group: org | tech | domain — determines node color and base size
fields.links[].type: uses | enables | operates — semantic relationship type
fields.links[].directed: boolean — true renders an arrowhead at the target node

_schema.replace: Add or remove nodes freely. Every node must appear in at least one link. Adjust weight to reflect relationship strength. Keep total node count below ~60 to avoid the hairball problem — above that, consider a chord diagram, arc diagram, or adjacency matrix instead.

nodes.label: UN OCHA
nodes.description: UN Office for the Coordination of Humanitarian Affairs. Central coordinator across all crisis response sectors.

nodes.label: World Food Programme
nodes.description: Delivers food assistance and supports food-insecure populations globally.

nodes.label: UNHCR
nodes.description: UN refugee agency. Protects and assists forcibly displaced people worldwide.

nodes.label: Médecins Sans Frontières
nodes.description: Provides emergency medical care in conflict zones and crisis settings.

nodes.label: World Health Organization
nodes.description: Leads global public health response and disease surveillance.

nodes.label: UNICEF
nodes.description: Focuses on children's rights, health, education, and protection in emergencies.

nodes.label: Oxfam
nodes.description: Addresses poverty and inequality through WASH and food security programming.

nodes.label: ICRC
nodes.description: International Committee of the Red Cross. Protection and humanitarian assistance in armed conflict.

nodes.label: Satellite Analysis
nodes.description: Remote sensing and satellite imagery analysis to assess damage, population movement, and land conditions.

nodes.label: Predictive Modelling
nodes.description: Statistical and ML models that forecast crisis onset, food insecurity, and displacement patterns.

nodes.label: Crisis Mapping
nodes.description: Real-time geospatial data aggregation for situational awareness and resource routing.

nodes.label: NLP Processing
nodes.description: Natural language processing of field reports, social media, and news for signal extraction.

nodes.label: Computer Vision
nodes.description: Image classification and object detection applied to medical imagery and satellite photos.

nodes.label: Biometric ID
nodes.description: Identity verification systems for beneficiary registration and deduplication.

nodes.label: Data Integration
nodes.description: Platforms that aggregate and normalise heterogeneous data sources across organisations.

nodes.label: Food Security
nodes.description: Ensuring consistent access to sufficient, safe, nutritious food.

nodes.label: Medical Response
nodes.description: Emergency healthcare, disease control, and surgical response in crisis settings.

nodes.label: Shelter
nodes.description: Provision of temporary and transitional housing for displaced populations.

nodes.label: Displacement Tracking
nodes.description: Monitoring and analysing population movement and returnee flows.

nodes.label: Protection
nodes.description: Safeguarding individuals from violence, exploitation, and human rights violations.

nodes.label: WASH
nodes.description: Water, Sanitation, and Hygiene programming in crisis and post-crisis settings.
